mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in INDPRO signals improving real output and earnings breadth; higher INDPRO momentum should be followed by positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value 101.9621 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month INDPRO momentum has nonpositive Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than half of 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, the higher discount-rate burden reduces equity valuation support, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 4.48 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or average triggered return is not below unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum accelerates while FEDFUNDS is already high, markets price a longer restrictive policy path, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation crosses above its 12-month inflation rate and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value 317.685 and FEDFUNDS 4.48
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate inflation-acceleration trigger has average 20-day returns greater than or equal to unconditional average returns over walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals deteriorating labor demand and recession risk; when unemployment rises sharply, expected earnings weaken and S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days turn negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its 3-month prior value by more than 0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value 4.1 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment-rise episodes deliver positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or have no negative return differential versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month RSAFS growth captures resilient consumer demand and supports revenue expectations; stronger retail sales momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value 724609.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS 3-month momentum has Rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day S&P 500 returns across two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A large decline in HOUST signals weakening rate-sensitive demand and future construction activity; when housing starts break materially lower, S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days are negative.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average by more than 10%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value 1289.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside-break triggers are followed by nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or no increase in left-tail return frequency.

mechanism_narrative: Falling INDPRO combined with rising CPIAUCSL identifies stagflation pressure: weaker real activity and higher input costs compress margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth crosses below 0 while CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation crosses above its 12-month inflation rate
failure_condition: Reject if joint INDPRO-contraction and CPI-acceleration episodes do not underperform unconditional 60-day returns by at least 1 percentage point on average.

mechanism_narrative: Positive PAYEMS momentum with UNRATE at or below 4.2% signals a soft-landing labor backdrop in which income growth supports demand without recession stress, lifting S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value 159536 and UNRATE 4.1
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-positive low-UNRATE trigger has 5-day forward win rate below 50% or average return below the unconditional 5-day average.
